name: Laguna del Arquillo Loop from Los Chospes
name_original: Laguna del Arquillo — circular desde Los Chospes
description: Hike the challenging 11.9-mile Laguna del Arquillo circular route from Los Chospes, exploring a unique karstic lake and diverse forests.
region_intro: Hiking around Robledo offers a variety of trails through diverse landscapes. The region features routes that traverse varied terrain, including areas with waterfalls and historical sites. Hikers can explore paths that lead through natural reserves and past significant landmarks. The trails provide opportunities to experience the local environment and its unique characteristics. Best hiking trails around Robledo The most popular hiking route is Alcaraz, Cascada de Los Batanes, Ruinas del Monasterio del Santo — circular en la Microrreserva La Malata y Los Batanes, a 11.8 miles (19.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 25 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant elevation gain and passes through a micro-reserve. Another top favourite among local hikers is Laguna del Arquillo Loop from Los Chospes, a difficult 11.9 miles (19.2 km) path. This trail offers a substantial distance and elevation, making it a challenging option for experienced hikers. Local hikers also love the Cerro de Santa Bárbara, Cascada de los Batanes, Castillo de Alcaraz — circular desde Alcaraz, a 13.3 miles (21.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 6 hours 12 minutes. Hiking around Robledo is defined by its diverse natural areas, including waterfalls and historical points of interest. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Robledo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 9 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Robledo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in the Robledo Mountains?
A: The Robledo Mountains offer a variety of hiking experiences with over 8 routes documented on komoot. These trails cater to different skill levels, from moderate to difficult, allowing hikers to explore the unique desert landscapes.

Q: What kind of landscapes and natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Robledo?
A: Hikers in the Robledo Mountains will encounter the rugged beauty of the Chihuahuan Desert, characterized by striking limestone cliffs, dramatic narrow canyons, and intricate slot canyons. The terrain includes arroyos, caves, and gentle slopes, with desert vegetation like creosote bush, mesquite, and diverse cacti. Many points offer amazing 360-degree vistas of the Rio Grande Valley.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Robledo?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate to difficult, the region does offer easier options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1083960418/alcaraz-cascada-de-los-batanes-ruinas-del-monasterio-del-santo-circular-en-la-microrreserva-la-malata-y-los-batanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Slot canyon loop — Las Cruces&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, family-friendly trail. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1083960418/alcaraz-cascada-de-los-batanes-ruinas-del-monasterio-del-santo-circular-en-la-microrreserva-la-malata-y-los-batanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geronimo's Cave Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 1.3-mile hike offering desert views and access to a historic cave.

Q: What are some challenging hikes in the Robledo Mountains?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the Robledo Mountains offer several difficult routes. Notable options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1083960418/alcaraz-cascada-de-los-batanes-ruinas-del-monasterio-del-santo-circular-en-la-microrreserva-la-malata-y-los-batanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaraz, Cascada de Los Batanes, Ruinas del Monasterio del Santo — circular en la Microrreserva La Malata y Los Batanes&lt;/a&gt;, an 11.8-mile trail with significant elevation gain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068310175/laguna-del-arquillo-loop-from-los-chospes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Arquillo Loop from Los Chospes&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 11.9-mile path.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Robledo?
A: Yes, many of the trails in the Robledo Mountains are designed as circular routes, offering varied scenery without retracing your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084064520/cerro-de-santa-barbara-cascada-de-los-batanes-castillo-de-alcaraz-circular-desde-alcaraz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de Santa Bárbara, Cascada de los Batanes, Castillo de Alcaraz — circular desde Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaraz – Cascada de los Batanes loop from Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see while hiking in the area?
A: The Robledo Mountains are rich in attractions. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4080509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prehistoric Trackways National Monument&lt;/a&gt;, renowned for 280-million-year-old fossilized footprint megatrackways. Other points of interest include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4080509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5919080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor and Towers of Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2971463" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viaduct on the Via Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls in the Robledo hiking area?
A: Yes, the region features routes that traverse areas with waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1083960418/alcaraz-cascada-de-los-batanes-ruinas-del-monasterio-del-santo-circular-en-la-microrreserva-la-malata-y-los-batanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Los Batanes&lt;/a&gt; is a notable waterfall mentioned in several trail names, offering a picturesque natural feature along your hike.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in the Robledo Mountains?
A: Given its location within the Chihuahuan Desert, the Robledo Mountains are best explored during the cooler months. Spring, fall, and winter offer more comfortable temperatures for hiking, allowing you to fully enjoy the serene and picturesque landscapes without the intense heat of summer.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Robledo?
A: The hiking trails in Robledo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from 9 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural areas, including waterfalls and historical points of interest, as well as the variety of options for different ability levels.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the trailheads in Robledo?
A: The Robledo Mountains are located just four miles northwest of Las Cruces, making them an accessible outdoor escape. While specific public transport routes to individual trailheads are not detailed, its proximity to the city suggests that local transport or a short drive would be the primary means of access.

Q: Are there any specific regulations or permits required for hiking in the Robledo Mountains?
A: The Robledo Mountains are part of the larger Organ Mountains-Desert Peaks National Monument. While general hiking is usually permitted, it's always advisable to check the official Bureau of Land Management (BLM) website for any specific regulations, permits, or advisories, especially for sensitive areas like the Prehistoric Trackways National Monument. You can find more information at &lt;a href="https://www.blm.gov/visit/robledo-mountains-wilderness" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;blm.gov&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: This path is known as "Sendero de Los Chospes" or "Route of Los Chospes". The route begins in the center of the town and is approximately 6 kilometers long. During the tour, you can enjoy the nature and landscape of the area, as well as some of the most outstanding monuments of Los Chospes, such as the church of San Juan Bautista or the old flour mill.

A wetland with a surface area of 522ha, recognized as a natural monument of Castilla-La Mancha. Next to it is another smaller lagoon. The vegetation of the area corresponds to a forest of junipers and holm oaks. Recommended visit at any time of the year.

This tunnel, known as "Túnel de Los Chospes", is about 400 meters long and was built at the beginning of the 20th century. It is necessary to take some precautions, since in some sections the tunnel can be dark and slippery.

faqs.question: Is the Laguna del Arquillo circular trail from Los Chospes suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, Komoot rates this specific circular route from Los Chospes as difficult. It requires a very good physical condition, sure-footedness, and appropriate footwear. While some direct routes to the lagoon are simpler, this 19 km loop with over 240 meters of elevation gain is considered challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Laguna del Arquillo circular hike from Los Chospes?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends near Los Chospes, so you would typically look for parking options within or close to the village to begin your circular route. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked for visitors accessing the natural monument.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse. You'll find sections following a Vía Verde, which are generally accessible paths. However, the full circular route also includes varied terrain, passing through well-preserved savin and holm oak forests. The path around the lake itself features karstic formations and aquatic vegetation.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The centerpiece is the stunning Laguna del Arquillo, a karstic lake with serene, clear waters. You'll also pass through the Los Chospes Trail and potentially the Los Chospes Tunnel. Beyond the lake, look for the Ojos de Villaverde, an important wetland, and geological formations of Jurassic dolomites.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter around Laguna del Arquillo?
faqs.answer: The area is rich in biodiversity. Around the lake, you might spot amphibians like the San Antonio frog and the European pond turtle, as well as aquatic birds such as the Western marsh harrier, kingfisher, and various ducks. The surrounding forests are home to carnivorous mammals like otters, badgers, wildcats, and genets.

faqs.question: Is the Laguna del Arquillo — circular desde Los Chospes trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Natural Monument generally welcomes visitors, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash to protect the local wildlife, especially given the presence of protected species and nesting birds. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike in the Laguna del Arquillo Natural Monument?
faqs.answer: The Laguna del Arquillo is recognized as a Natural Monument of Castilla-La Mancha. Generally, access to natural monuments for hiking does not require a specific permit or entrance fee, but it's always advisable to check for any local regulations or restrictions, especially concerning group sizes or specific activities.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike the Laguna del Arquillo circular route?
faqs.answer: The area's natural beauty can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, while winter might bring colder weather, though the lake's colors change throughout the day regardless of season.

faqs.question: Does this circular trail connect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Sendero de los Serranos, the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz, and various segments of the Camino de Los Chospes al Arquillo.

faqs.question: Are there any local legends associated with Laguna del Arquillo?
faqs.answer: Yes, local folklore suggests that the Laguna del Arquillo is bottomless, adding a mysterious charm to its natural beauty. This legend highlights the deep connection locals have with this unique natural monument.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this difficult hike?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and nearly 19 km length, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear with good ankle support, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing. A map or GPS device is also recommended, as is a first-aid kit.
